name: PALESTINE CHILDRENS RELIEF FUND
mission: We provide free medical care and humanitarian aid for any child in the Middle East, regardless of their religion or politics.
programs: Huda Al Masri Pediatric Cancer Department
theoryOfChange: Donations fund medical and humanitarian relief (treatment, surgeries, supplies, food, water, shelter) and support rebuilding healthcare facilities and psychosocial services (trauma counseling/mental health), aiming to improve access to quality medical care for children in the Middle East/Levant.

The organization provides transparent financial reporting and annual reports.

The organization provides food vouchers and medical treatments through international cooperation.
PCRF facilitates specialized medical interventions and food parcel distribution.
The organization distributes vegetable baskets and provides critical medical interventions.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: PCRF demonstrates strong financial health and high alignment for donors seeking to support urgent global health needs, though its impact score reflects the high costs associated with specialized surgical interventions.
areas_for_improvement: High cost per beneficiary for specialized medical interventions
Dependency on volatile regional stability for program delivery

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization shows high alignment with donor interests through its transparent Zakat policy and focus on the urgent cause of global health in conflict zones [2][4]. With total revenue of $94,873,840, PCRF has established a significant track record in providing critical medical interventions and community-centered infrastructure enhancement [3][6].
dimension_explanations.impact: PCRF achieves significant outcomes through medical missions and the distribution of essential supplies, such as food parcels and vegetable baskets [7][8]. While the organization maintains a high program expense ratio of 88.1%, the direct service model for specialized medical care results in a higher cost per beneficiary compared to general aid [3][5].

The organization is recognized as Zakat-eligible, maintaining a dedicated fund to support eligible beneficiaries through various humanitarian programs [2].

impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Tracking outcomes ensures that specialized medical missions result in successful long-term health recoveries for children.

strengths.detail: The organization demonstrates remarkable fiscal discipline, spending only <cite id="1">$0.02 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions.
PCRF maintains <cite id="1">40.9 months of working capital</cite>, providing a massive buffer to sustain operations during regional instability.
The charity holds a <cite id="1">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>, the highest level of disclosure for non-profit organizations.

strengths_deep_dive: PCRF's operational strength lies in its ability to scale rapidly during emergencies while maintaining high efficiency. In 2023, they managed <cite id="3">$90,655,454 in public contributions</cite>, utilizing a <cite id="1">88.1% program expense ratio</cite> to deliver aid. Their impact data shows a high volume of direct service, including <cite id="6">196,650 meals provided</cite> through partnerships and <cite id="6">1,074 children treated</cite> during medical missions in a single year. This is supported by an <cite id="1">independent board of 11 members</cite> ensuring governance oversight.
rich_narrative.summary: The Palestine Children's Relief Fund (PCRF) operates as a critical humanitarian bridge, providing <cite id="5">free medical care and aid</cite> to children across the Middle East. The organization distinguishes itself through a dual-model approach: facilitating <cite id="5">specialized medical missions</cite> that bring international expertise to local hospitals and <cite id="6">sponsoring one-time surgeries</cite> for children who cannot be treated locally. In 2023, the charity experienced a massive surge in scale, reporting <cite id="3">$92,196,846 in total revenue</cite>, a significant increase from previous years driven by urgent regional crises. Despite this rapid growth, PCRF maintained <cite id="1">a program expense ratio of 88.1%</cite> according to recent financial filings, ensuring that resources are directed toward its core mission of pediatric health. Their impact is quantifiable through diverse interventions, ranging from <cite id="7">1,946 specialized medical interventions</cite> to the distribution of <cite id="7">52,449 food parcels and vegetable baskets</cite> to families in need. Beyond direct medical care, PCRF invests in long-term sustainability by <cite id="1">training local doctors and building hospital infrastructure</cite>, such as the Huda Al Masri Pediatric Cancer Department. The organization is also a vital resource for Muslim donors, maintaining a <cite id="2">verified Zakat Fund</cite> that directs 88.1% of eligible donations to <cite id="2">asnaf categories including the poor and needy</cite>.
